title: Ensure that the API server pod specification file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive

input_row.title: Ensure that the API server pod specification file permissions are set to 644 or more restrictive
input_row.description: Ensure that the API server pod specification file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.rationale: The API server pod specification file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the API server. You should restrict its file permissions to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be writable only by the administrators on the system.
input_row.audit: OpenShift 4 deploys two API servers: the OpenShift API server and the Kube API server. The OpenShift API server delegates requests for Kubernetes objects to the Kube API server. The OpenShift API server is managed as a deployment. The pod specification yaml for openshift-apiserver is stored in etcd. The Kube API Server is managed as a static pod. The pod specification file for the kube-apiserver is created on the control plane nodes at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver-pod.yaml. The kube-apiserver is mounted via hostpath to the kube-apiserver pods via /etc/kubernetes/static-pod-resources/kube-apiserver-pod.yaml with permissions 0644. To verify pod specification file permissions for the kube-apiserver, run the following command. #echo “check kube -apiserver pod specification file permissions” for i in $( oc get pods -n openshift-kube-apiserver -l app=openshift-kube-apiserver -o name ) do oc exec -n openshift-kube-apiserver $i -- \ 17 | P a g e stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/static-pod-resources/kube-apiserver-pod.yaml done Verify that the permissions are 644 or more restrictive.
input_row.remediation: No remediation required; file permissions are managed by the operator.
input_row.default_value: By default, in OpenShift 4, the kube-apiserver-pod.yaml file has permissions of 644.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Access the OpenShift Container Platform CLI (oc) on your local machine or a management terminal.
2. Execute the following command to retrieve the names of the OpenShift API server pods:
   ```
   oc get pods -n openshift-kube-apiserver -l app=openshift-kube-apiserver -o name
   ```
3. For each pod name retrieved, execute the following command to check the file permissions of the kube-apiserver pod specification file:
   ```
   oc exec -n openshift-kube-apiserver <pod_name> -- stat -c %a /etc/kubernetes/static-pod-resources/kube-apiserver-pod.yaml
   ```
   Replace `<pod_name>` with the actual name of each pod you found in step 2.
4. Verify the output of the command in step 3. Ensure that the permissions returned are `644` or more restrictive, i.e., `0644` or `0600`.
5. If the permissions are greater than `644`, document the non-compliance and note the need for remediation, even though the operator manages permissions.

**EXPECTED SECURE CONFIGURATION:** The kube-apiserver pod specification file should have filesystem permissions set to `644` or more restrictive, ensuring it is writable only by designated administrators. 

**HOW TO CONFIRM COMPLIANCE:** Compliance is confirmed if all checked pods return a permission setting of `644` or lower. If any pod's permissions exceed `644`, it indicates a compliance issue that needs attention.
